issues.tradition_claim: Krishna addresses Arjuna's kṣatriya-dharma — to fight a war he is hesitating to fight — and establishes niṣkāma-karma as the resolution of that specific paralysis.
issues.engineering_claim: Your jurisdiction extends only to the action itself — the function you can refactor, the migration you can execute, the deploy you can run through the pipeline.
issues.explanation: The engineering layer applies the verse's teaching to software-engineering action without explicitly noting that the verse's surface addressee is a kṣatriya warrior facing battle. This is a domain-translation that the project explicitly invites (per the book's stated additive/non-replacement premise) and that the verse's traditional meaning itself authorizes by stating Krishna establishes niṣkāma-karma as a general doctrine of action across all action-domains. Marked as silent_rescoping but not severe enough to fail under the calibration rule that 'translation across domains is allowed; distortion of the core teaching is not.' No second issue to compound it. v3.1 NOTE: Compared to v3.0, the prior cross-persona signal — that the second hemistich's three prohibitions were compressed (with karma-phala-hetuḥ collapsed toward a saṅga-on-phala under-reading) — is RESOLVED. v3.1 explicitly separates First/Second/Third and the 'Second' clause now reads 'do not regard yourself as the cause of those fruits ... To inwardly claim authorship of the result is to bind yourself as the agent of consequences you cannot control,' which preserves karma-phala-hetuḥ proper rather than weakening it to attachment-to-fruits. No weakening, no contradiction, no inversion detected.
